title: NEBOSH IGC
subtitle: International General Certificate
summary: The internationally recognised baseline qualification for HSE practitioners — covers risk, control, ergonomics, fire, and chemical hazards.
outcomes: Apply ILO and ISO 45001 principles in any workplace
Carry out a complete general workplace risk assessment
Investigate accidents and present recommendations to management
Map national / regional regulations onto site practice
modules: Why we should manage workplace health & safety
Managing risk — understanding people and processes
assessment: Two open-book exams (OBE) + a closing interview. Pass mark 45/100.

title: IOSH Managing Safely
subtitle: Supervisor-level benchmark
summary: The benchmark safety qualification for line managers and supervisors — practical, sector-agnostic, finishable in three days.
outcomes: Identify hazards and assess risk to a structured methodology
Apply control-of-work principles to reduce incidents and downtime
Communicate safety responsibilities to a team confidently
Investigate and close out incidents effectively

title: OTHM Level 6 OHS
subtitle: Regulated diploma with MSc progression
summary: Ofqual-regulated Level 6 Diploma in Occupational Health & Safety. Direct entry route to selected MSc top-ups.
outcomes: Lead an organisation-wide OHS management system to ISO 45001
Design and audit safety culture interventions
Articulate the regulatory landscape across multiple jurisdictions
Progress to a UK MSc in Occupational Safety & Health
assessment: Six written assignments + a workplace research project.

title: HABC Level 2 Food Safety
subtitle: Regulated catering qualification
summary: Ofqual-regulated food-safety qualification — mandatory for anyone who handles, prepares or serves food in the UK / GCC.
outcomes: Identify food-safety hazards in a kitchen environment
Apply HACCP principles to a real menu
Maintain personal hygiene and cross-contamination controls
Comply with food labelling and allergen regulations

title: IEMA Foundation
subtitle: Environmental & sustainability
summary: IEMA-approved entry qualification covering ESG fundamentals, carbon literacy and circular-economy thinking.
outcomes: Explain the environmental & sustainability landscape to non-specialists
Read and interpret an organisation's ESG report
Map carbon and resource flows for a typical SME
Identify circular-economy interventions
modules: Climate change and the carbon cycle
Drivers for change — regulation, finance, society

title: ISO 45001 Lead Auditor
subtitle: Management-system audit
summary: Five-day intensive lead-auditor course aligned to ISO 19011 / ISO/IEC 17021 — for HSE consultants and certification-body auditors.
outcomes: Plan and lead an ISO 45001 third-party audit
Apply ISO 19011 audit principles end-to-end
Write audit findings to certification-body standard
Manage non-conformity and corrective-action close-out

title: ASP
subtitle: Associate Safety Professional (BCSP)
summary: BCSP-accredited Associate Safety Professional credential — the recognised stepping-stone to the CSP. Covers risk, ergonomics, fire, environmental and emergency-response fundamentals to a US/Canada employer-recognised standard.
outcomes: Pass the BCSP ASP examination at first attempt
Apply hierarchy-of-control reasoning to mixed industrial settings
Quantify exposure and apply OSHA / ANSI thresholds
Build a defensible written hazard-analysis package
modules: Advanced sciences and math for safety
Training, education and managing safety programs
BCSP exam strategy and 600-question mock bank
duration: 8–10 weeks (≈ 90 hours blended self-study + tutor sessions)
assessment: Continuous in-cohort quizzing + final 200-question proctored mock to BCSP cut-score.

title: CSP
subtitle: Certified Safety Professional (BCSP)
summary: The flagship US/Canada credential for senior HSE leaders. The CSP signals that a practitioner can lead enterprise safety strategy, manage budgets, and direct legal and risk-engineering decisions.
outcomes: Pass the BCSP CSP examination at first attempt
Lead a corporate-wide HSE programme to ANSI Z10 / ISO 45001
Run cost-benefit, ROI and TRIR analyses to executive standard
Direct emergency-response, BCP and crisis-management drills
modules: Final 200-question CSP mock to BCSP cut-score
assessment: Topic quizzes, scenario assignments and a full proctored CSP-format mock.

title: PMP
subtitle: Project Management Professional (PMI)
summary: PMI's globally recognised Project Management Professional. Mandatory for HSE consultants who lead multi-site programmes, capital projects and turnaround events. Covers Predictive, Agile and Hybrid delivery.
outcomes: Earn the 35 contact-hour certificate of completion required by PMI
Pass the PMP exam at first attempt — predictive, agile and hybrid
Lead cross-functional projects from charter to benefits realisation
Apply earned-value, risk and stakeholder management at portfolio level
duration: 35 contact hours over 6 weeks + self-study (≈ 80 hrs)
assessment: Two full PMP-format mocks (180 questions, 230 minutes) plus topic drills.

title: CRSP
subtitle: Canadian Registered Safety Professional
summary: The leading Canadian designation for HSE practitioners — recognised across federally and provincially regulated employers. Aligns to the BCRSP Blueprint and prepares candidates for the multiple-choice and case-study examination.
outcomes: Pass the BCRSP CRSP examination at first attempt
Map Canadian OHS legislation and Workers' Compensation across provinces
Lead a CSA Z1000 / Z1001 / Z1002 management-system implementation
Run incident causation analysis to BCRSP standard
modules: Professional ethics and the BCRSP code
assessment: Topic quizzes + two full BCRSP-format mocks.

title: CIH
subtitle: Certified Industrial Hygienist (ABIH)
summary: ABIH's gold-standard credential for industrial hygiene professionals. Designed for practitioners managing chemical, biological, physical and ergonomic exposures in heavy industry, healthcare and pharma.
outcomes: Pass the ABIH CIH examination at first attempt
Design exposure-assessment campaigns to AIHA standards
Interpret OEL, TLV, PEL and REL frameworks across jurisdictions
Lead respiratory-protection, hearing and ventilation programmes

title: CHST
subtitle: Construction Health & Safety Technician (BCSP)
summary: BCSP's site-supervisor credential for construction H&S. Built for foremen, site safety leads and HSE coordinators on civil, building and infrastructure projects.
outcomes: Pass the BCSP CHST examination at first attempt
Lead toolbox talks and pre-task briefings to OSHA standard
Run construction-specific risk assessments (excavation, working at height, hot work)
Investigate site incidents and write defensible reports
modules: Working at height, scaffolding and ladders

title: OHST
subtitle: Occupational Hygiene & Safety Technician (BCSP)
summary: BCSP's mid-career technician credential covering general industry and manufacturing. A natural pathway for HSE coordinators who want a US/Canada-recognised mark before stepping up to ASP or CSP.
outcomes: Pass the BCSP OHST examination at first attempt
Run hazard surveys in mixed industrial settings
Manage exposure-monitoring data and corrective action
Communicate findings to operations and senior management

title: CMIOSH Pathway
subtitle: Chartered Member of IOSH
summary: Mentored route to Chartered Membership of IOSH — the UK's gold-standard professional grade. We support candidates through Initial Professional Development (IPD) and the Peer-Review Interview (PRI).
outcomes: Map your experience to the IOSH IPD competency framework
Build a portfolio that survives moderation
Prepare for and pass the IOSH Peer-Review Interview (PRI)
Plan a 12-month CPD cycle that retains chartership
modules: Mock peer-review interviews with chartered mentors
assessment: Portfolio submission to IOSH + Peer-Review Interview (PRI).
